origin.name: Poland
origin.death_registration: Urząd Stanu Cywilnego (Civil Registry Office) within 3 days
origin.key_document: Akt zgonu (death certificate from Urząd Stanu Cywilnego)
origin.source: FCDO Poland travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Warsaw guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Poland is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler and less costly than full body repatriation. You will need the local death certificate, cremation certificate, and an urn transport declaration.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Akt zgonu (death certificate from Urząd Stanu Cywilnego)
Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Poland is a Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered at the local Urząd Stanu Cywilnego within 3 days.
The Prokuratura (Prosecutor's Office) orders a post-mortem for suspicious or unnatural deaths.
All Polish-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Polish documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Warsaw and consulates in Krakow and Gdansk can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Poland to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Poland. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Akt zgonu process, apostille, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
